messages.content: You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Submit reads q.closed and sends on the jobs channel without holding q.mu.RLock(), so a concurrent Shutdown can flip closed and close(q.jobs) mid-send, producing a data race on q.closed and a possible send-on-closed-channel panic. Keywords: data race, missing read lock, send on closed channel, concurrent shutdown, panic
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Store.Put acquires a read lock (RLock) instead of a write lock (Lock) while inserting into the codes map, so concurrent shorten requests cause a data race and can trigger a fatal concurrent map write. Keywords: data race, rlock instead of lock, concurrent map write, put method, write lock needed, sync.rwmutex misuse
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In AllowN the availability check uses a strict greater-than (`b.tokens > need`) instead of `>=`, an off-by-one that wrongly denies a request that would exactly exhaust the available tokens (e.g. AllowN(5) on a full bucket of capacity 5 returns false). Keywords: off-by-one, allown availability check, should be >= not >, strict greater-than, exact tokens wrongly denied, boundary equal case
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Get no longer acquires c.mu, so it reads from (and deletes expired entries out of) the shared items map without synchronization, creating a data race with concurrent Set/Get/DeleteExpired calls. Keywords: data race, get does not acquire the mutex, missing c.mu.lock in get, concurrent map read and write, delete from map without holding the lock
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In GroupSum the accumulator is declared as `var sums map[string]int` (a nil map) instead of `make(map[string]int)`, so the first `sums[key] += v` write panics with "assignment to entry in nil map" for any input that has at least one data row. Keywords: nil map, assignment to entry in nil map, var sums instead of make(map[string]int), groupsum panics at runtime, map not initialized before write
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Publish reads the shared subs map and sends on subscriber channels without acquiring b.mu (the RLock/RUnlock pair was dropped), creating a data race with Subscribe and Close that can corrupt the map and panic by sending on a channel Close is concurrently closing. Keywords: data race, publish does not hold the lock, missing rlock/runlock, concurrent map read and write, send on closed channel, go test -race
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Transfer no longer acquires l.mu.Lock()/Unlock(), so concurrent calls read and mutate the shared balances map and history slice without synchronization, producing a data race, lost updates, and possibly a fatal concurrent-map-write panic. Keywords: data race, missing mutex lock, transfer does not acquire l.mu.lock, unsynchronized access to balances map, concurrent map writes can panic, lost update on balances and history
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

The capacity-overflow check in Put uses `c.ll.Len() >= c.capacity` instead of `> c.capacity`, an off-by-one that evicts the least-recently-used entry one insertion too early so the cache only ever holds capacity-1 entries (and 0 when capacity is 1). Keywords: off-by-one, eviction condition, len() >= c.capacity should be > c.capacity, evicts one entry too early, cache holds capacity-1 entries, put overflow check
